title: The Phase One Deal Not Dead In The Water
section_path: The Phase One Deal Not Dead In The Water
section_path_text: The Phase One Deal Not Dead In The Water
summary:
Main
By Ulf Bergman
The future status of the trade deal concluded between China and the US a bit over a year ago has been debated, with a new US administration taking over the reins. During the first year of its existence, compliance with it was rather underwhelming with Chinese purchases failing to meet the volumes implied. According to data compiled by the Peterson Institute for International Economics, actual Chinese imports of US goods covered by the deal amounted to 99.9 billion dollars in 2020 versus the 159.0 billion dollars implied by the agreement.
